description: Mama Ejima home cooking restaurant is well structured spot where you get home food away from home. Specialized in dishing all kinds of Nigerian soups and swallows, different types of cooked rice and drinks
address: Maputa Street in Neighborhood Market at Wuse 3
dish_1_description: Soup source from bitter leave and fermented cassava that has been cooked, pounded to pasta
dish_2_description: Delicious soup from melon seeds, taken with cassava flakes turned to pasta
dish_3_description: Jollof Rice is a unique kind of Nigerian cooked rice blended and fused with tomato sauce with ingredients like peppers, chilli peppers onions and seasonings

description: Gub's Kitchen Ventures is local restaurant that also serves amazing dishes: White Rice, fried rice, Egusi soup, afang soup, vegetable soup, oha soup, okra soup, Plus other soups. They have Garri, semo, pounded Yam and other swallows
address: Sakode Street opposite PDP Office in Wuse Zone 5, Abuja.
dish_1_description: Semo with Egusi is a popular Nigerian Meals. A semolina pasta eaten with Egusi soup, a soup source from melon seeds, grinded and garnished with some kinds of fish and assorted meat
dish_2_description: A boiled white Rice taken with tomato sauce. This sauce has mashed fresh tomatoes, vegetable oil, onions, pepper, and seasonings ingredients
dish_3_description: Pounded Yam is a paste from boiled and pounded Yam, eaten with soup source from okra. Okra is edible vegetable plant plant that is slimy in nature and very nutritious, chopped and cooked with leaves, some soups ingredients

description: Prepares and fries Beans cakes popularly known as Akara alongside with pap. Mama Jemila make masah-- a popular northern cuisine. Here you can also get other fried foods like potatoes, and yams
address: Maputa Street in Neighborhood Market at Wuse Zone 3 close to FRSC Headquarters Abuja
dish_1_description: Akara is a deep fried beans Cake made from a mashed and ground Beans pudding
dish_2_description: Fried Yams are sliced Yams or chunked and fried in a deep hot vegetable oil till it softs
dish_3_description: Fried potatoes are sliced potatoes or chunked and fried in a deep hot vegetable oil till it softs

description: Oga Munkaila fast-food and Meishai is fast food and breakfast meals Spot where you can enjoy a tasty and garnished instant noodles prepared in different ways. The place also serves, toast, fried eggs, Arabian tea, English tea, noodles with eggs, toast(bread)with egg, milk and chocolate hot drinks and soft drinks
address: Wuse Zone 4, Constantine Street, intersection with shinyanga Street, Abuja.
dish_1_description: Indomie with Eggs is fast-food parboiled and fried noodles with ingredients like vegetable oil, carrots, green pepper, Bell peppers, onions and seasonings, then topped with fried Eggs.
dish_2_description: Toast with eggs is a fried eggs that is placed inside a cut or sliced bread, then heat the bread in a frying pan or toaster till the bread turns brownish
dish_3_description: This is form of hot beverage sourced from the Arabian medicinal boiled leaves. The drink can preferably have : honey, sugar,. milk, chocolate, stirred and served hot.

vendor_name: Madam Cynthia Roasted yams, plantains nd Beans
description: Madam Cynthia Roasted yams plantains and Beans is a nice place to enjoy your brunch, lunch with a well roasted yams, plantains, and porridge beans. This roasted yams or plantains can be mixed together without the porridge beans and sprinkled with palm oil sauce. Also the plantain or Yam can be served with oil sauce differently
address: The behind or back of U.B.A Branch at Zone 3, very close to Febson Mall along Sultan Abubarka Way, Abuja
dish_1_description: This is a porridge beans and a roasted plantains chopped in chunks and then mixed in company of vegetables oil sauce that has some onions, pepper and seasonings ingredients. It can go with some fish or cowskin (kpomo). It's a delicious and tasty local meal.
dish_2_description: This is a porridge beans and a roasted yams cut in chunks and then mixed in company of vegetables oil sauce that has some onions, pepper and seasonings ingredients. It can go with some fish or cowskin (kpomo). It's a delicious and tasty local meal
dish_3_description: It's a meal served with roasted chunks of yam and vegetable oil sauce separately by dunking your yams in the sauce This oil sauce has ingredients like hot peppers, onions, crayfish and seasonings. The oil also charred.

description: Lajawa Suya and Barbecue is a well structured spot that has existed more than 30 years. This place is the right place for lovers of grilled Chicken, meats, gizzards, Lamb and all kinds of barbequed or suyaed meats. It's quite pocket friendly and their service is also a top notch in Suya and grill business.
address: Constantine Street beside Zone 4 Plaza within the bureau de change area in Zone 4 Abuja
dish_1_description: Chicken Suya is grilled or barbeque chicken meat which is prepared by heating the chicken on a grill stove or structure with firewood or coal underneath it. Before then, the chicken is completely smeared with a popular Suya pepper and seasonings called Yaji in Hausa language. The Chicken Suya can be served with the Yaji, accompanied with some cucumber, onions, cabbage and other vegetables
dish_2_description: The Gizzards is a chicken part, usually the the stomach. This part is really delicious and uniquely tasty. It's usually eaten with the seasoned Pepper(Yaji) and some Veggies
dish_3_description: Suyaed meats are various kinds of grilled or barbequed meats like Beef, Mutton, and Chevon served with seasoned Pepper along side with some vegetables like the cabbage, carrots, onions

description: Sells beef , chicken, and ram suya with masa and gizzard
dish_1_description: Sells beef , chicken, and ram suya with masa and gizzard
dish_2_description: Sells beef , chicken, and ram suya with masa and gizzard

description: Budget friendly restaurant that dishes local delicacy and cuisine, like Amala, Fufu, Garri and soups like Egusi, vegetable soups, Yoruba soup, fried rice, white Rice and noodles(Spaghetti)
address: Matadi Street, besides Tamara Guest Inn, located at Neighborhood Market zone 3
dish_1_description: Amala is a Yoruba food pasta from Yam flour or flakes prepared by mixing the flour in hot water, stirring and lightly cooked to form pasta
dish_2_description: White Rice is a staple and common food rice cereal consumed with tomato sauce or any other desired sauce like vegetable or egg
dish_3_description: Spaghetti is a long pasta noodle prepared with ingredients like tomato, pepper, onion, crayfish, seasonings and vegetable like carrots, cabbage, spinach, etc

description: NativePot and Healthy Meals deals strictly on local delicacy especially Igbo tribe native foods and some local meals you can get from Igbo villages like Abacha, akidi, fio-fio, etc.
address: Matadi Street beside Tamara Guest Inn along Abidjan Street located at Wuse 3
dish_1_name: Okpa, Abacha with Ugba, Yam with Akidi, Fio-fio, Cowheadbetc
dish_1_description: Okpa: This is the proteinous cowpeas pudding. The cowpeas is grinded and mixed ingredients like palm oil, pepper, onion, crayfish, seasoning, vegetable, etc,. Then wrapped either in a plastic bag or plate and cooked
dish_2_description: Abacha: This is a slices of parboiled cassava. Thoroughly washed and then mixed with ingredients like palm oil, assorted fishes, meats, stock fishes, cowskin, crayfish, onion and seasonings. Akidi: Akidi is an Igbo native food from cocoyam source. This cocoyam is boiled, chopped, sun dried and pounded then cooked with local ingredients like Ukpaka(oil bean), vegetable oil, onion, crayfish, fish or meat plus seasonings
dish_3_description: Abacha: This is a slices of parboiled cassava. Thoroughly washed and then mixed with ingredients like palm oil, assorted fishes, meats, stock fishes, cowskin, crayfish, onion and seasonings. Ugba is a local name for oil bean which serve as the garnishing or complementary ingredient
